Record Class RagbotShell

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
com.embabel.examples.ragbot.RagbotShell
Record Components:
luceneSearchOperations - search/index component backed by Lucene and shared by chat and ingestion
@ShellComponent record RagbotShell(com.embabel.agent.rag.lucene.LuceneSearchOperations luceneSearchOperations) extends Record
Exposes Spring Shell commands for ingestion, inspection, and maintenance of the local RAG store.

This record is intentionally thin: each command delegates to LuceneSearchOperations and related Embabel ingestion helpers. Keeping shell commands simple is a good practice because it makes behavior easy to test and easy to reuse from other interfaces.

  • Constructor Details

    • RagbotShell

      RagbotShell(com.embabel.agent.rag.lucene.LuceneSearchOperations luceneSearchOperations)
      Creates an instance of a RagbotShell record class.
      Parameters:
      luceneSearchOperations - the value for the luceneSearchOperations record component

  • Method Details

    • ingest

      @ShellMethod("Ingest URL or file path: Ingests Schumann's music criticism by default") String ingest(@ShellOption(help="URL or file path to ingest",defaultValue="./data/schumann/musicandmusician001815mbp.md") String location)
      Ingests a single URL or file into the RAG store.

      If the input is a local file path, it is normalized to an absolute file URI before ingestion. If the path is a directory, this command returns a friendly message guiding the user to ingestDirectory(String).

      Parameters:
      location - url or local file path to ingest
      Returns:
      Returns ingestion outcome including document id or skip reason

    • ingestDirectory

      @ShellMethod("Ingest a directory of files") String ingestDirectory(@ShellOption(help="Directory path to ingest",defaultValue="./data") String directoryPath)
      Ingests all files in a local directory into the RAG store.

      Best practice for learning projects: keep validation close to user input (existence checks, file-vs-directory checks) so failure modes are clear and easy to debug from the command line.

      Parameters:
      directoryPath - local directory containing files to ingest
      Returns:
      Returns summary of how many files produced newly ingested documents

    • zap

      @ShellMethod("clear all documents") String zap()
      Deletes all indexed documents and chunks from the Lucene store.
      Returns:
      Returns confirmation message with number of deleted documents

    • chunks

      @ShellMethod("show chunks") String chunks()
      Prints all chunks and metadata currently stored in the index.
      Returns:
      Returns total chunk count after listing details to the console

    • sections

      @ShellMethod("show sections") String sections()
      Prints all indexed Section records.
      Returns:
      Returns total section count after listing section ids and titles

    • contentElements

      @ShellMethod("show content elements") String contentElements()
      Prints all indexed ContentElement instances.
      Returns:
      Returns total content element count after listing identifiers and types

    • info

      @ShellMethod("show lucene info: number of documents etc.") String info()
      Shows high-level diagnostics for the Lucene store.
      Returns:
      Returns textual stats including document and chunk counts

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
